page: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Xen hypervisor, this instance includes 488.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware feature is its bundled local storage, consisting of 15,200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The instance also provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core results. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes consistently register in the middle 50% tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations, which achieve 37.43 million operations per second, also align with average performance tiers.

The primary tradeoff of the i3.16xlarge is its reliance on an older Intel Xeon architecture, which limits single-threaded execution speeds and modern vector instruction sets like AVX-512. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high local storage density and substantial memory capacity, making it a viable option for workloads that prioritize massive local NVMe storage over raw single-core compute power. Appropriate workloads include large-scale databases, NoSQL stores, transactional databases, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance configured with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 488.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core capabilities, which rank in the bottom 10%, and multi-core performance, which reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and database operations also perform in the middle 50% tier. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-dense workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. It is best suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL systems, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance equipped with 64 vCPUs, 488 GB RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 488 GB RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 488.0 GB of system memory powered by Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processors
15,200 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data access
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Xen hypervisor
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance paired with lower single-core efficiency
Suited for large-scale databases, NoSQL deployments, and static web serving
